loom.say: Ah. The funicular. And in front of it... that is a substantial gentleman.

fondant_moves.say: There now. A man who appreciates butter is never truly a henchman.

look.text: A potted palm, leaning hard into the sea wind. It has chosen a difficult career.
Far below, the sea files its complaint against the cliff. The cliff declines to settle.

use.text: You lean on it rakishly. Nobody sees. The ambience notes it, at least.

look.text: Twin cables climbing the dark towards the Baron's tea room. Taut as the small talk up there.
The Baron's private funicular, brass and velvet — and one immovable Mr. Fondant standing squarely between you and its little door.

use.text: You'd have to get past Mr. Fondant first, and Mr. Fondant is a solved problem for exactly nobody so far.
A gentleman blocks the way with the patience of geology. Best say hello first.

look.text: A load-bearing wall in a bowler hat. His tie is a novelty print of tiny biscuits — the single known clue to the man within.

use.text: You consider moving him bodily. Physics declines to be consulted.

itemUse.text: You present the shortbread with both hands, as protocol surely demands.

look.text: Mr. Fondant stands aside, dabbing one eye with a handkerchief the size of a pillowcase. The shortbread is nowhere to be seen. The wrapper is folded, with reverence, into his breast pocket.

exits.name: salon doors
exits.look: Back into the warm hum of the salon.

exits.name: funicular
exits.look: The Baron's private funicular. Brass, velvet, and a grade no sensible railway would attempt.
